## [00:00 - 03:00]

continuing on looking at ideas for kind of stores and in particular around optimization, what I want to show you here is just picking out one of the stores that I've been analyzing as part of this list. The first store on the list is a company called House from London and what I've done is to plug them into Pingdom and also GTmetrics. So GTmetrics and Pingdom are the two main free test sites that I use. We do have paid accounts as well, but you can go through the test process as well to show you what potentially you're aiming for and what potentially good and bad experience and site looks like. Now I have just randomly picked House because it was on the top, it was literally the top site that came up on this particular listing. No other reasons. So looking at the results and I pre-run this because sometimes there is a five or ten minute wait depending on how many people in the queue. The performance grade and the load time are two of the things that I'm looking at. So generally for a performance grade I'm looking for a C which shows that I've got most of the things in place that help to improve page performance. At the moment I've got a D on this particular page and a 66. A load time of 1.21 is generally when the page starts to appear in the browser and is kind of starting to load aside from all the code and other things that are happening in the background. 1.21 is okay, it's not the fastest. We've got client right now that's around half of that with a ton of optimizations. This is a Shopify store. The client with half the speed of that is actually on an Econ platform called Magento and there are many many others out there as well. But you know one of the things that I would be looking at and we do this for every project we're working on. So whether it's you know my own info products or my own Econ stalls or with clients one of the early steps of optimizations we want to do is to find ways to get that page loading faster. Because when that page loads faster the user benefits the cost potential customer in terms of their experience and also you benefit as an advertiser because Facebook reward you with lower CPMs. The lower CPMs also allow you to get the best users out of the auction and therefore you get the best auction wins. So don't sleep on landing page optimization and also funnel as well. So it's not just making sure that the landing page is as fast as possible but it's making sure those pages down the funnel are also optimized for speed as much as possible. There may be cases where for example your brand has like big images on the home page and therefore you need to kind of think about things slightly differently. But you know tools like this give you an idea to think about what you can do to actually improve your performance as well.

## [03:00 - 06:02]

This is something which for some non-technical people you might need some help and that's absolutely fine. So again if you've got a developer in-house get them to run this. If you're working with an external tech agency get them to run this and to get you your performances optimizes it can be to give you the best chance of reducing the CPMs through optimization and increasing your conversion rate as well. What I would say is one of the old methods of managing your pixel would be to delay the pixel fire until maybe five or ten seconds so that your pixel would not pick up people who are bouncing or bots or anything like that. The problem with delaying your whole pixel fire is this. How does Facebook know how long your page takes to load? The only way it knows is based on the pixel. So if you artificially increase that load time from 1.21 seconds to 5 or 10 seconds then guess what Facebook look at that data and say right this user clicked at this time and it took five seconds for the pixel to load on the site they clicked into. That then feeds back to Facebook that this is a low quality user experience so you may be flagged in your ads policy to say it is a low quality user landing page experience or you may be and or you may be punished with higher CPMs and potentially lose out on better auctions as well and also it's a bad customer experience if your page is genuinely loading that late as well. But if you want to delay the pixel event that's a completely different ballgame so to clarify with the Facebook pixel you have a piece of code which sends data to Facebook do not delay that but you can delay the actual event that sent so for example the view content event the atta car initiate check out for example use an external tool like Google Tag Manager or coding it yourself if you want to eliminate people who are bouncing so a bouncer could be someone who visits your landing page and disappears within 10 seconds generally it's 30 seconds if you fire it after that and you know for example that people landing on your product page it will take them at least 30 seconds to read the content choose their variant and can add to cart then you can be confident in saying that if I delay the view content pixel on my product page or my landing page and people that then fire it after 30 seconds hit my pixel data that's going to be higher quality so that that's that part of it's fine you can also create additional events within the pixel as well which we've talked about earlier in the BPM method so for example people have scrolled a certain percentage of your page people have spent a certain time as well like you can fire a pixel every

five seconds every 30 seconds for example to look at engagement so there are different ways of doing this but don't delay the actual pixel code from firing because that will be a detriment to you as things like that on some of the apps it looks like they're installed one thing I'd really encourage you to be careful of is installing too many Shopify or e-commerce platform apps so you know stores like e- like Shopify like WooCommerce etc. they make it so easy for you to add plugins to your store that people add things without thinking about the impact on the user experience I've seen stores where we've audited or taken on a client where we've gone through the audit process and the amount of apps that they have installed is severely slowing down the loading of the page to the extent that you know when we do an audit and we're looking to improve performance and things like that that there have been occasions that all we've done is to remove unnecessary plugins and what I mean by that is it's not just the case of saying oh you're not allowed to have that plugin but some plugins carry more weight on your page than they need to so every plugin designer whichever platform you're on is load times as well so it's really important to make sure that you are looking at this from a technical point of view whatever your product is your services what kind of ads you're running make sure that the technicalities of your funnel of your landing page are a consideration because that will make a big big difference to generating lower costs higher conversion rates and getting the best out of the auction as well
